name: Smoking
recipeIngredient: Herb prunings or stems from fresh herbs
recipeInstructions:
1. Follow the basic instructions that came with your smoker.
2. Adapt any marinade, rub, or paste recipe for smoking.
3. For water smokers, vary the liquid used in the pan.
4. Place herb prunings, herb stems, dried citrus peels, onion skins, celery tops, and other aromatic scraps in the fire pan before starting.
5. Pre-rub meat with dry rubs the night before to allow flavors to penetrate.
6. Place seasoned meat in a plastic bag with herbs.
7. Start the fire when ready, typically allowing it to burn throughout dinner preparation.
8. Maintain the fire by emptying the fire pan into the Weber grill and adding fresh coals as needed.
9. Use mostly hardwood such as grapevine prunings, crabapple, oak; avoid soft woods like pine.
10. Fuss with the smoker only every two hours or less frequently.
11. Enjoy fish, beef, pork, and chicken smoked to your preference.
Kathy
